title: HTTP Server Throughput Analysis (ThousandEyes)
dataSources: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream ‚Äî Metrics (HTTP Server tests)
spl:
`stream_index` thousandeyes.test.type="http-server"
| stats avg(http.server.throughput) as avg_throughput by thousandeyes.test.name, thousandeyes.source.agent.name
| eval throughput_mbps=round(avg_throughput/1048576,2)
| sort -throughput_mbps
description: Measures download throughput from ThousandEyes agents to web servers, revealing bandwidth constraints or content delivery issues from the user perspective.
value: Measures download throughput from ThousandEyes agents to web servers, revealing bandwidth constraints or content delivery issues from the user perspective.
implementation: The OTel metric `http.server.throughput` reports bytes per second. The Splunk App Application dashboard includes an "HTTP Server Throughput (MB/s)" line chart. Low throughput combined with high latency typically indicates a network bottleneck; low throughput with low latency suggests a server-side rate limit.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream ‚Äî Metrics (HTTP Server tests).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
The OTel metric `http.server.throughput` reports bytes per second. The Splunk App Application dashboard includes an "HTTP Server Throughput (MB/s)" line chart. Low throughput combined with high latency typically indicates a network bottleneck; low throughput with low latency suggests a server-side rate limit.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`stream_index` thousandeyes.test.type="http-server"
| stats avg(http.server.throughput) as avg_throughput by thousandeyes.test.name, thousandeyes.source.agent.name
| eval throughput_mbps=round(avg_throughput/1048576,2)
| sort -throughput_mbps
```

Understanding this SPL

**HTTP Server Throughput Analysis (ThousandEyes)** ‚Äî Measures download throughput from ThousandEyes agents to web servers, revealing bandwidth constraints or content delivery issues from the user perspective.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream ‚Äî Metrics (HTTP Server tests). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Invokes macro `stream_index` ‚Äî in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by thousandeyes.test.name, thousandeyes.source.agent.name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **throughput_mbps** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (throughput MB/s over time), Table (test, agent, throughput), Column chart by agent.
visualization: Line chart (throughput MB/s over time), Table (test, agent, throughput), Column chart by agent.
